idea: Build the 24-chapter master outline around compute, data, algorithms, institutions, products, and agents.
evidence_hypothesis: A strong architecture prevents encyclopedia drift and creates prize-book momentum.

idea: Create the source map for the LLM era through 2026-05-24, starting with primary sources, papers, company posts, talks, benchmark pages, repos, and major reporting.
evidence_hypothesis: Investigative quality depends on source topology before prose volume.

idea: Draft the ChatGPT chapter as the narrative ignition point, then backfill origins around it.
evidence_hypothesis: Opening from the product shock can give general readers immediate stakes.

idea: Draft the Claude Code and coding agents chapter as the moment software begins to write software at industrial scale.
evidence_hypothesis: Coding agents are central to the LLM story and can connect architecture to lived developer workflow.

idea: Design the visual grammar: timelines, model family trees, scaling curves, datacenter/power diagrams, architecture schematics, and benchmark landscapes.
evidence_hypothesis: A consistent visual language will make the book feel designed, not assembled.

idea: Create the model-rankings appendix from arena/provider tables with source snapshots and access dates.
evidence_hypothesis: Leaderboards and provider economics are a factual spine for the 2026 landscape.

idea: Use GTC-2026-Keynote.pdf to build a NVIDIA GTC 2026 source/asset pack, distinguishing happened facts from announced roadmaps by the cutoff.
evidence_hypothesis: The deck can supply high-impact visuals and hardware narrative if provenance is precise.

idea: Propose 5 alternate prize-book titles and subtitles, then test them against FT/Pulitzer/Goodreads/canon criteria.
evidence_hypothesis: The title changes the perceived seriousness and memorability of the whole artifact.
